chapter: Sulla's Dictatorship, Proscriptions, and Constitutional Reforms (82-79 BCE)

questions.question: Where did Sulla land when he returned to Italy from the East in 83 BCE?
What title did Sulla's soldiers give him during his eastern campaigns, meaning 'the Fortunate'?
Which battle on 1 November 82 BCE decided the Roman civil war in Sulla's favor?
What were the proscriptions?
options: New tax laws imposed on the provinces after the civil war
Religious decrees issued by the Senate to purify Rome after civil conflict
Published lists of citizens declared enemies of the state, subject to death and property confiscation
Military recruitment notices posted across the Italian peninsula

questions.question: What was the name of the law that formally granted Sulla his unlimited dictatorship in late 82 BCE?
How old was Pompey when he raised three legions from his family's clients and joined Sulla's cause in 83 BCE?
In what year did Sulla resign the dictatorship and retire to private life?
What ended the First Mithridatic War in 85 BCE?
options: The capture and execution of Mithridates VI in Athens
A Senate decree ordering Sulla to withdraw from the East
The destruction of the Pontic fleet near Rhodes
The Peace of Dardanus, in which Mithridates surrendered his conquests and paid an indemnity

questions.question: How did Cinna, the leader of Rome's government during Sulla's absence, die in 84 BCE?
options: He was executed on Sulla's orders after being captured at sea
He died of illness while preparing to cross the Adriatic
He was killed by his own mutinous soldiers at Ancona
He was assassinated by a Sullan agent in the Forum

questions.question: On which field outside Rome's walls was Sulla cremated with a grand state funeral after his death in 78 BCE?
What bounty did Sulla offer for each proscribed person killed, according to Plutarch?
Which two men restored the full powers of the tribunate that Sulla had stripped away, during their joint consulship of 70 BCE?
